# Implementation Phases
## January 2026 – December 2031

## Phase 1: Needs Assessment and Stakeholder Engagement
### Months 1–6

| **Activity** | **Description** |
|--------------|------------------|
| Comprehensive needs assessments | Identify financial, networking, and business development needs |
| Surveys and focus groups | Conducted across key innovation hubs nationwide |
| Stakeholder mapping | Identify government bodies, private investors, development partners |
| Alignment workshops | Align SACCO services with sectoral demands |
| Baseline data collection | Establish benchmarks for future M&E |

## Phase 2: Institutional Framework and Governance Setup
### Months 6–12

| **Activity** | **Description** |
|--------------|------------------|
| Draft SACCO by-laws | Develop governance documents based on cooperative principles |
| Register with authorities | Ministry of Trade, UMRA, URSB |
| Establish governance structures | Elect Board, Credit Committee, Supervisory Committee |
| Open bank accounts | Institutional accounts for operations |
| Establish countrywide branches | Kampala, Mbarara, Gulu, Mbale |

## Phase 3: Product and Service Design
### Months 9–15

| **Activity** | **Description** |
|--------------|------------------|
| Market needs assessments | Validate findings from Phase 1 |
| Co-create products with early members | Involve potential members in design |
| Develop loan packages | Seed capital, working capital, equipment financing |
| Design savings products | Regular savings, fixed deposits, youth schemes |
| Create non-financial services | Mentorship, incubation, training programs |

## Phase 4: Digital Platform Development
### Months 6–18

| **Activity** | **Description** |
|--------------|------------------|
| Procure/customize SACCO MIS | Select and adapt a management information system |
| Develop mobile/web interface | User-friendly platforms for members |
| Integrate mobile money | Enable seamless payments and disbursements |
| Test and deploy | Pilot with early members, refine based on feedback |
| Train staff and members | Ensure smooth adoption |

## Phase 5: Capital Mobilization and Funding
### Ongoing from Month 6

| **Activity** | **Description** |
|--------------|------------------|
| Membership drive | Recruit founding members, collect share contributions |
| Investor engagement | Pitch to angel networks, VCs, impact investors |
| Grant applications | Apply for innovation and cooperative grants |
| Government partnerships | Engage Ministry of ICT, Ministry of Finance |
| Diaspora outreach | Target Ugandans abroad for investment |

## Phase 6: Member Recruitment and Onboarding
### Ongoing from Month 12

| **Activity** | **Description** |
|--------------|------------------|
| Branding and recruitment strategy | Develop marketing materials |
| Outreach campaigns | Partner with universities, hubs, associations |
| Digital and physical registration | Facilitate easy enrollment |
| Issue member IDs and share certificates | Formalize membership |

## Phase 7: Service Delivery and Network Facilitation
### Ongoing from Month 12

| **Activity** | **Description** |
|--------------|------------------|
| Lending operations | Begin loan disbursement |
| Mentorship programs | Match startups with experienced mentors |
| Networking events | Organize tech summits, pitch days, expos |
| Training workshops | Deliver financial literacy and business skills programs |

## Phase 8: Monitoring, Evaluation, and Continuous Improvement
### Ongoing

| **Activity** | **Description** |
|--------------|------------------|
| Design M&E framework | Establish KPIs and data collection systems |
| Digital dashboards | Real-time tracking of key metrics |
| Periodic assessments | Quarterly reviews, annual surveys |
| Feedback loops | Member input mechanisms |
| Iterate services | Adjust based on lessons learned |
